subject: UX & Research
title: Plain Language Movement
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Plain Language Movement.

hook.text: A government form says 'heretofore.' A rewrite says 'before now.' Same meaning, half the confusion.

buildup.text: Plain language uses short sentences, common words, and clear structure. No jargon, no legalese.

discovery.text: The VA rewrote a benefits letter from college level to 8th-grade. Comprehension jumped 80%.

twist.text: Plain language isn't dumbing down. It removes jargon and shortens sentences. Experts prefer it too.

climax.text: Test readability with Hemingway Editor or Flesch-Kincaid. Aim for grade 6-8 for public-facing content.

punchline.text: Short words, short sentences, no jargon. Plain language is accessible language.

quiz.question: What happened when the VA rewrote a benefits letter in plain language?
options: Comprehension jumped 80% when rewritten from college level to 8th-grade reading level
Veterans complained the letter was too simple
Processing time doubled due to the extra pages
